Deployed across all of OpenAI’s largest NVIDIA GB200 supercomputers, including stargate (OCI Abilene, Texas) and Microsoft Fairwater
Used to train multiple frontier models for ChatGPT and Codex
Multiple link flaps per minute between T0-T1 switches: zero measurable impact on synchronous training
Four-tier-1 switch reboots during a frontier model training: no coordination needed with training teams
GPU interface link failure: job survives with reasonable performance (slowdown < capacity loss)

Comparison: Before vs After MRC






























EventBefore MRCWith MRCLink flap between T0-T1Training disruptionNo measurable impactSwitch rebootCareful coordination requiredNo coordination neededGPU NIC link failureTraining job failsJob survives, recoversLink repairMust drain/disable firstRepair while in service
Protocol Foundation
MRC extends RDMA over Converged Ethernet (RoCE), an IBTA standard. It draws on techniques from the Ultra Ethernet Consortium (UEC) and extends them with SRv6-based source routing.
Open Source
Released as an OCP contribution: MRC Specification (opens in new window)
Accompanying paper: “Resilient AI Supercomputer Networking using MRC and SRv6”
Related

multi-plane-networks — the topology foundation of MRC
srv6-source-routing — the routing approach MRC uses
stargate — OpenAI’s supercomputer using MRC
openai — the organization behind MRC
open-compute-project — where MRC is open-sourced

concepts/multi-plane-networks.title: Multi-Plane Networks
concepts/multi-plane-networks.content:
Multi-Plane Networks
A multi-plane network splits each high-speed network interface into multiple smaller links connected to different switches, creating parallel independent networks (planes).
In MRC
In mrc, a single 800Gb/s network interface is split into eight 100Gb/s links, each connected to a different switch. This creates eight parallel 100Gb/s networks instead of one 800Gb/s network.
Key Benefits








































PropertyConventional (single-plane)Multi-PlaneGPU scale (2-tier)Limited131,000+ GPUsSwitch tiers needed3-42Power consumptionHigherLowerComponent countHigherLowerPath diversityLimitedHigh (more local traffic to T0)Failure resilienceSingle failure can disruptRedundancy built in
Why Two Tiers Matters
A switch with 64 ports at 800Gb/s becomes a switch with 512 ports at 100Gb/s. This radical increase in port count per switch means a two-tier fabric can connect over 100,000 GPUs — something that would require 3-4 tiers conventionally. Fewer tiers = fewer components that can fail, less power, lower cost.
The Utilization Challenge
Multi-plane networks have massive path diversity, but traditional protocols (like RoCE) require each transfer to follow a single path so packets arrive in order. This creates:

Flow collisions on the same link → congestion
Each flow using only one plane → wasted capacity

MRC solves this with packet spraying — spreading a single transfer’s packets across hundreds of paths simultaneously.
Related

mrc — the protocol that fully utilizes multi-plane networks
stargate — supercomputer built with multi-plane networking

concepts/output-trust.title: Output Trust
concepts/output-trust.content:
Output Trust
A verification system for LLM-generated answers in knowledge bases. When an LLM answers a user query, the answer is a generated claim — not a verified fact. Without safeguards, wrong answers get indexed and pollute future queries. Output trust quarantines new outputs until they pass verification.
Introduced by sage-wiki.
Verification Pipeline

Quarantine — New answers are written to wiki/under_review/ as “pending”
Consensus — Same question + same answer from different source chunks accumulates confirmations. Independence scored via Jaccard distance between chunk sets
Grounding — sage-wiki verify checks claims against source passages via LLM entailment
Promotion — When both thresholds met → promoted to wiki/outputs/ → indexed into search

Configuration
trust:
  include_outputs: verified     # "false" | "verified" | "true" (legacy)
  consensus_threshold: 3        # confirmations for auto-promote
  grounding_threshold: 0.8      # min grounding score (0.0-1.0)
  similarity_threshold: 0.85    # cosine similarity for question matching
  auto_promote: true
Key Properties

Source-change awareness — when sage-wiki compile modifies cited sources, confirmed outputs are automatically demoted
Conflict resolution — outputs resolve promotes one answer and rejects competing ones
Staleness cleanup — outputs clean --older-than 90d removes abandoned pending outputs

Why This Matters for Agent Systems
In multi-agent or team setups, one agent’s hallucination can poison the shared knowledge corpus. Output trust ensures that only verified, consensus-backed answers enter the searchable index — critical for coding-agents and any system where agents share a persistent knowledge base.
Related Concepts

sage-wiki — the tool that implements output trust
tiered-compilation — complementary: trust verifies answers, tiers scale the pipeline
catastrophic-forgetting — analogous concern: just as neural networks can forget, knowledge bases can be polluted
heuristic-learning — Heuristic Learning’s regression testing addresses a similar problem for heuristic systems

concepts/rate-reduction.title: Rate Reduction (Coding Rate Reduction)
concepts/rate-reduction.content:
Rate Reduction (ΔR)
The central theoretical framework of deep-representation-learning as presented in deep-representation-learning-book (Chapters 4–6). Rate reduction is an information-theoretic objective that unifies representation learning under a single principle.
Definition
For a representation Z of data X, the coding rate reduction is:

ΔR(Z) = R(Z) − Σⱼ R(Zⱼ)

Where:

R(Z) = coding length (in bits) required to encode the entire dataset’s representations
Σⱼ R(Zⱼ) = sum of coding lengths to encode each class/subset independently
ΔR = the “savings” gained by encoding jointly vs. separately

Intuition





















When ΔR is…MeaningLargeClasses are well-separated, intra-class compact → good representationSmall/ZeroClasses overlap or are diffuse → poor representationNegativeClasses are more similar to each other than to themselves → degenerate
Maximizing ΔR simultaneously promotes:

Intra-class compression: Points in the same class have similar representations
Inter-class discrimination: Points in different classes are far apart

Why It Works
The rate reduction objective emerges naturally from lossy coding theory. If we want to compress data efficiently, we should assign fewer bits to predictable (low-variance) structures and more bits to unpredictable (high-variance) ones. A good representation makes data within each class predictable (compressible) while keeping classes distinct.
As Unifying Principle
Many seemingly unrelated methods are revealed as special cases or approximations of maximizing ΔR:

































MethodRelationship to ΔRPCAMaximizes ΔR for Gaussian distributionsICAMaximizes ΔR under independence constraintsContrastive LearningApproximates ΔR via pairwise comparisonsAutoencodersImplicitly maximize ΔR through bottleneckK-meansDiscrete approximation of ΔR maximizationTransformer attentionGradient step toward sparse ΔR maximization
Mathematical Connection to white-box-transformers
When ΔR is extended to a sparse version (with a sparsity-promoting regularizer), its gradient steps can be computed explicitly. Each step corresponds to one layer of a Transformer:

Query-Key-Value → emerges from the gradient of the coding rate
Softmax attention → approximates the sparsity projection operator
Feed-forward layers → implement the unrolled iterative optimization (ISTA/LISTA-like)

Connection to closed-loop-transcription
Rate reduction serves as the objective for both the encoder and decoder in the closed-loop framework:

Encoder f: Maximizes ΔR(Z) for compactness and discrimination
Decoder g: Minimizes reconstruction error to ensure sufficiency
Closed-loop check: Re-encoding X̂ should produce the same Z — verified by ΔR consistency

Key Source
deep-representation-learning-book, Chapters 4 (introduction), 5 (unrolled optimization interpretation), and 6 (closed-loop application).
Cross-References

deep-representation-learning — The overarching paradigm
compression-representation — Compression as learning mechanism
white-box-transformers — Architectural consequence of ΔR optimization
closed-loop-transcription — Self-consistency built on ΔR
yi-ma — Primary architect of the framework

concepts/schank-abelson-cognitive-lingui.title: Schank & Abelson Cognitive Linguistics
concepts/schank-abelson-cognitive-lingui.content:
Schank & Abelson Cognitive Linguistics
Roger Schank and Robert Abelson developed foundational theories in cognitive linguistics and AI during the 1970s-80s at Yale. Their work provides the theoretical backbone for modern structured representations of agent knowledge, including SSL.
Three Key Frameworks
1. Conceptual Dependency (CD)
A theory of natural language understanding based on a small set of semantic primitives — primitive actions (e.g., PTRANS for physical transfer, ATRANS for abstract transfer) and state changes. The goal was to represent the meaning of any sentence in a language-independent form.
2. Script Theory
Introduced in “Scripts, Plans, Goals, and Understanding” (1977). A script is a stereotypical sequence of events for a familiar situation (e.g., “going to a restaurant”). Scripts have:

Slots — roles (customer, waiter)
Props — objects (menu, food)
Entry conditions — preconditions for the script
Scenes — sequential sub-parts of the event
Results — post-conditions

3. Memory Organization Packets (MOPs)
Introduced in “Scripts, Plans, Goals, and Understanding” and refined in “Dynamic Memory” (1982). MOPs organize related scripts and episodes in memory, enabling:

Reminding — similar past experiences are recalled when relevant
Generalization — common patterns across episodes are abstracted
Indexing — experiences are stored with retrieval cues

Relevance to SSL
The SSL framework explicitly draws on all three:

























Schank & AbelsonSSL LayerMappingMOPs (organization)SchedulingWhen to invoke, inter-skill ordering, episode indexingScript Theory (structure)StructuralControl flow, scenes, sub-steps, slotsConceptual Dependency (primitives)LogicalConcrete actions, resource usage, state changes
Legacy
Schank & Abelson’s work was foundational to:

Case-based reasoning
Early expert systems
Modern cognitive architectures
And now, structured representations for LLM agent skills

Related

ssl-representation — modern application of these theories
coolpku — the research group carrying this lineage forward

concepts/scientist-digital-twin.title: Scientist Digital Twin
concepts/scientist-digital-twin.content:
Scientist Digital Twin
A Scientist Digital Twin is an AI system that digitally replicates a specific scientist’s cognitive assets — knowledge, analytical paradigms, methodological toolkits, experiential judgment, and academic taste — making them queryable, inferable, and transmissible beyond the individual.
Unlike general AI assistants or autonomous AI scientists (which aim to discover new knowledge), a Scientist Digital Twin targets cognitive preservation and augmentation of a specific human expert.
Core Architecture
Five-layer model:



































LayerFunctionKey TechnologiesData CollectionIngest papers, reports, talks, emails, reviews, lab notebooksPDF extraction, OCR, speech-to-textKnowledge ExtractionParse entities, relations, methodologies from raw sourcesLLM-assisted NER + RE, ontology alignmentKnowledge RepresentationThree-tier KG: Factual KG + Methodological KG + Experiential KBNeo4j/NebulaGraph, GraphRAGReasoning EngineKG-enhanced RAG + Multi-Agent orchestration + workflow templatesLangGraph, MCP protocolInteraction LayerQ&A, collaborative analysis, report generation, teachingNatural language + citation tracing
Three Knowledge Graphs
Factual KG: Domain entities and their relationships. E.g., [Topological Insulator] → predicted-by → [Kane & Mele, 2005]
Methodological KG: The scientist’s analytical workflow templates. Each node is a decision procedure with input conditions, analysis steps, judgment criteria, common pitfalls, and canonical cases.
Experiential KB: Specific decision stories — what happened, what the scientist did, why, and the outcome. Both successes and failures are equally valuable.
Differentiation from AI Scientist



































DimensionAI Scientist (Lu et al.)Scientist Digital TwinGoalAutonomous discoveryCognitive preservation + augmentationKnowledge sourceGeneral academic corpusOne specific scientist’s outputStyleStandardizedPreserves individual academic styleReasoningEnd-to-end generationReplicates the scientist’s analytical paradigmsEvaluationDiscovery noveltyAlignment with scientist’s actual judgment
The AI Scientist (Nature 2026, ai-scientist) demonstrates that LLM-based systems can autonomously navigate the full research lifecycle — idea generation, experimentation, manuscript writing, peer review. The Scientist Digital Twin applies related agentic architectures but inverts the purpose: not to replace the scientist, but to amplify and perpetuate their unique cognitive approach.
Challenges

Tacit knowledge extraction: Scientists often cannot articulate their judgment process. Requires think-aloud protocols and case tracing.
Knowledge currency: Scientific knowledge evolves. Needs continuous paper ingestion + KG versioning.
Reasoning explainability: Cannot be black-box. Must show reasoning chains with source citations.
Ethical boundaries: Consent, privacy, intellectual legacy control, and preventing misuse.

Related

ai-scientist — the Nature paper demonstrating end-to-end automated research
agent-memory — theoretical framework for storing experiential knowledge in agents
skill-ontology — three-layer framework for organizing reusable capabilities (analogous to methodological KG)
llm-wiki-compiler — knowledge compilation pattern that can serve as the documentary layer
heuristic-learning — alternative paradigm where software structure replaces neural parameters (relevant for encoding expert heuristics)

concepts/skill-coevolution.title: Skill Coevolution
concepts/skill-coevolution.content:
Skill Coevolution
The joint optimization of three coupled agent skill capabilities — selection, utilization, and distillation — toward a shared task-outcome objective, rather than optimizing each in isolation.
Introduced by skill1.
The Three Capabilities

























CapabilityWhat it meansLearned fromSelectionWhich skill to use for this task?Low-frequency trend of task outcomeUtilizationHow to execute with the chosen skill?Task outcome signalDistillationHow to extract a new skill from this trajectory?High-frequency variation of task outcome
Why Coevolution Matters
Optimizing these capabilities independently leads to conflicts:

A skill selected well but utilized poorly → wasted retrieval
A skill utilized well but never distilled → no library growth
Distillation without good selection → library fills with noise

Skill1 shows that a single reward signal is sufficient when its frequency components are decomposed: the slow trend credits selection quality, while fast variation credits distillation quality.
Contrast with Other Approaches



































ApproachSelectionUtilizationDistillationskill1 (coevolution)Jointly optimizedJointly optimizedJointly optimizedskill-routing + agentRetrieved top-kAgent decidesManualskill0 (internalization)Not neededIn weightsTraininglifelong-skill-evolutionSequentialSequentialPer-task patch
Related Concepts

skill1 — the framework that implements coevolution
skill-internalization — alternative: bypass the library entirely
lifelong-skill-evolution — sequential evolution across task families
skill-representation — the representation quality affects all three capabilities

concepts/skill-evaluation.title: Skill Evaluation
concepts/skill-evaluation.content:
Skill Evaluation
Multi-dimensional quality assessment framework for AI agent skills, introduced by SkillNet. Addresses the problem that without systematic evaluation, skill repositories are prone to “pollution” — executability, safety, and robustness assessed only indirectly through downstream task success introduces technical debt.
Five Dimensions (SCEMA)



































DimensionQuestionAssessment MethodSafetyHazardous operations? Prompt injection resistant?LLM rubric evaluationCompletenessAll critical steps covered? Prerequisites explicit?LLM rubric evaluationExecutabilityActually runs in sandbox? Hallucinated tool calls?LLM + sandbox executionMaintainabilityModular? Local updates without breaking?LLM rubric evaluationCost-awarenessTime latency? Resource consumption? API costs?LLM rubric evaluation
Scoring
Each dimension scored on three levels: Good, Average, Poor.
Quality distribution in SkillNet’s 150K+ curated skills:

Safety and Maintainability have the highest proportion of “Good” ratings
Executability is the hardest — largest fraction of “Average” ratings, reflecting rigorous filtering

Evaluation Pipeline

Automated LLM evaluator — GPT-5o-mini with fine-grained rubrics for Safety, Completeness, Maintainability, Cost-awareness
Sandbox execution — For Executability: skills with code/tool invocations are actually run in controlled environments
Human validation — Periodic random sampling with PhD-level CS annotators for calibration

Human Alignment Validation
On 200 randomly sampled skills with 3 PhD-level CS annotators:

MAE < 0.03 across all dimensions
QWK ≈ 1.000 (near-perfect)
Confirms LLM-based evaluation is human-aligned, robust, and scalable

Why Evaluation Matters
Without systematic evaluation:

Skill repositories accumulate broken/unsafe skills (“pollution”)
Agent trust degrades — agents can’t distinguish reliable from unreliable skills
Technical debt compounds — bad skills cause failures that are hard to attribute
No quality signal for skill selection at runtime

Related Frameworks

























FrameworkApproachDimensionsSkillNetAutomated LLM + sandbox5 (SCEMA)Terminal-BenchHuman-written canonical solutions + strict pass/fail1 (correctness)SkillFlowTask success rate on downstream benchmarks1 (utility)
SkillNet is the most comprehensive — it’s the only one that evaluates skills intrinsically rather than through proxy task performance.
Related

skillnet — the infrastructure implementing this evaluation
skill-ontology — quality-gated entry into the skill ontology
skill-routing — evaluation scores could inform retrieval ranking
skill-representation — evaluation feeds back into skill design quality
terminal-bench — complementary evaluation for terminal agents
skillflow — downstream task success as evaluation proxy

concepts/skill-graph.title: Skill Graph
concepts/skill-graph.content:
Skill Graph
A directed multigraph 𝒢 = (Ω, 𝒦) where nodes (Ω) are scenarios and edges (𝒦) are skills, each pointing from a precondition scenario to a postcondition scenario. A directed path in the graph corresponds to a sequential agentic workflow, with each skill’s postcondition serving as the precondition of the next. Introduced by skillsynth.
Why Scenario-Mediated?
Unlike skill-only topologies (e.g., SkillNet which models direct skill-to-skill connections), a scenario-mediated graph captures the execution state between skills. This enables:

Controllable trajectory diversity — paths explicitly specify both what skills to exercise and what intermediate scenarios to traverse
Compositional realism — paths represent real-world workflows where scenarios connect meaningfully
Coverage optimization — inverse-frequency sampling steers the empirical distribution toward uniform coverage over the scenario × skill product space

Construction Pipeline (SkillSynth)

Skill filtering — Only skills that are: executable on Linux, structured workflows, deterministic, safe
Scenario inference — LLM infers plausible pre/post-condition scenarios from skill specifications
Deduplication — Hierarchical agglomerative clustering with Louvain-based coarse bucketing
Cross-skill alignment — Bidirectional LLM judging of semantic compatibility between postconditions of one skill and preconditions of another
Merging and filtering — LLM merges aligned pairs and filters valid transitions

Scale (SkillSynth graph)





































MetricValueScenario nodes82,073Skill transitions57,214LLM-verified bridges185,529Giant component size85.6% of nodesConnected components6,251Median node degree2Max node degree752
Path Sampling: Inverse-Frequency Weighting
Uniform random walks concentrate on high-degree hubs. SkillSynth uses inverse-frequency weighting: source scenarios and next skills are drawn with probability ∝ 1/(visit_count + 1). With monotone progression (no revisiting scenarios/skills within a path), this progressively steers toward uniform coverage, directly motivated by the learning objective (Equation 4 in the paper).
Related Topologies

SkillNet (Liang et al. 2026) — Skills as nodes in a relational graph with explicit inter-skill connections (not scenario-mediated). Four edge types: similar_to, belong_to, compose_with, depend_on. Part of a larger ecosystem with built-in skill evaluation and a 200K+ skill repository. skillnet
AgentSkillOS (Li et al. 2026) — Hierarchical capability tree with DAG-based orchestration
Voyager (Wang et al. 2023) — Reusable skill library for embodied agents
clawhub — Open skill registry that provides the raw skills for graph construction

Relationship to Hermes Skill System
The scenario-mediated skill graph is conceptually similar to how Hermes agent skills chain together: each skill’s output state (postcondition) becomes the input state (precondition) for the next tool call. The formalization in SkillSynth provides a theoretical framework for understanding why this composition works.
Related Concepts

skillsynth — the framework that constructs and uses skill graphs
skillnet — direct skill-to-skill relational graph (different topology, same domain)
skill-ontology — three-layer skill organization framework
terminal-agents — the agents trained on graph-sampled trajectories
skill-router — complementary: flat retrieval for skill selection vs. graph traversal
skill-coevolution — skill selection + utilization + distillation as a joint RL problem
skill-representation — broader problem of encoding reusable agent skills
ssl-representation — Scheduling-Structural-Logical representation, another approach to skill encoding
coding-agents — agents that can be trained using skill graph-sampled data

concepts/skill-internalization.title: Skill Internalization
concepts/skill-internalization.content:
Skill Internalization
The process of embedding agent skill knowledge into model parameters through training, so the agent can exhibit skilled behavior zero-shot — without loading any skill text at inference time.
Introduced by skill0.
Motivation
Inference-time skill augmentation has three fundamental limits:

Retrieval noise — irrelevant skills distract the agent
Token overhead — skill content consumes context budget
No true acquisition — the model follows instructions but doesn’t learn the skill

Skill internalization asks: can we train the model to become the skill, rather than carry it?
Mechanism (SKILL0)

Dynamic Curriculum: starts with full skill context → progressively withdraws → ends with zero-shot operation
On-policy helpfulness: each skill’s utility is evaluated during training; only beneficial ones are retained within a decaying budget
Compact visual context: skills grouped by category, rendered with interaction history, under 0.5k tokens/step

Contrast with Library-Based Approaches



































Library-Based (skill1)Internalization (skill0)Skills at inferenceLoaded from libraryIn model weightsContext costHigh (full skill text)NoneUpdate mechanismAdd/remove from libraryFine-tune modelRetrieval dependencyRequiredNoneGeneralizationLimited to retrieved skillsPotential for novel combinations
Related Concepts

skill0 — the framework that implements skill internalization
skill1 — complementary library-based co-evolution approach
lifelong-skill-evolution — external skill maintenance (opposite direction)
catastrophic-forgetting — risk: internalizing new skills may overwrite old ones
skill-representation — what form of skill knowledge is most amenable to internalization
agent-memory — parametric memory is essentially skill internalization; the memory framework provides theoretical grounding

concepts/skill-ontology.title: Skill Ontology
concepts/skill-ontology.content:
Skill Ontology
A structured framework for organizing agent skills into a composable, queryable, and evolvable knowledge network. Introduced by SkillNet as a three-layer architecture that transforms isolated skills into a structured capability space.
Three-Layer Structure
Layer 1: Skill Taxonomy        ← Functional categorization
Layer 2: Skill Relation Graph   ← Inter-skill dependencies
Layer 3: Skill Package Library  ← Physical deployment units

Layer 1 — Skill Taxonomy
Organizes skills into a multi-level hierarchy using category and tag relations:

10 top-level categories: Development, AIGC, Research, Science, Business, Testing, Productivity, Security, Lifestyle, Other
Fine-grained tags: frontend, llm, physics, bioinformatics, etc.
Provides the high-level semantic skeleton for navigation and filtering

Layer 2 — Skill Relation Graph
Instantiates abstract tags into specific Skill Entities (e.g., Matplotlib, Playwright) with four typed edges:






























RelationMeaningUse Casesimilar_toFunctional equivalence, interchangeableRedundancy detection, replacement, robustnessbelong_toSub-component of a larger workflowHierarchical abstraction, modularizationcompose_withFrequently co-invoked (output → input)Workflow composition, pipeline generationdepend_onPrerequisite required for executionDependency tracking, safe execution planning
Edges form a directed, typed multi-relational graph — nodes are skills, edges encode structural dependencies and functional associations.
Layer 3 — Skill Package Library
Physical organization layer where individual skills are encapsulated into Skill Packages (e.g., data-science-visualization) via the packaged_in relation. Facilitates modular release and deployment.
Dynamic Nature
The ontology is dynamic, not static — tags can be continually added from the taxonomy, and LLMs infer relations from them, enabling instantiation of new edges as skills are added or updated. Experiential feedback (task outcomes, execution traces) refines relation weights over time.
Construction
Hybrid pipeline:

Semantic embedding matching — cosine similarity for candidate similar_to edges
LLM-based relational inference — prompted reasoning for compose_with, belong_to, depend_on
Execution trace alignment — co-occurrence patterns in agent trajectories

Comparison: SkillNet Ontology vs. SkillSynth Graph
| Aspect | SkillNet Ontology | SkillSynth Graph |
|--------|-------------------|----------------|
| Node type | Skills | Scenarios |
| Edge type | Skills (similar_to, belong_to, compose_with, depend_on) | Skills (as transitions) |
| Primary purpose | Organization, retrieval, quality management | Task synthesis via path sampling |
| Construction | LLM inference + embeddings + traces | Scenario inference + cross-skill alignment |
SkillNet’s ontology is more of a knowledge graph for skill management; SkillSynth’s graph is a state machine for trajectory generation. Both are valid and complementary.
Related

skillnet — the infrastructure that implements this ontology
skill-graph — SkillSynth’s scenario-mediated alternative
skill-evaluation — the quality framework that gates what enters the ontology
skill-representation — broader context: what skills ARE before they’re organized
ssl-representation — structured skill representation, different organizational approach
skill-router — retrieval from skill registries built on this ontology

concepts/skill-representation.title: Skill Representation (Agent Skills)
concepts/skill-representation.content:
Skill Representation (Agent Skills)
Skill representation is the problem of how to encode reusable agent capabilities — instructions, control flow, constraints, and tool calls — in a form that both humans and machines can effectively use.
Current State
Most agent systems (including Hermes Agent) represent skills as text-heavy markdown documents (SKILL.md). These documents embed:

Invocation interfaces (triggers, when to use)
Execution structure (steps, control flow)
Constraints and edge cases
Tool call specifications

The problem: all of these signals are entangled in a single textual surface. Machines must reason over natural language to extract structured knowledge.
The SSL Approach
SSL proposes disentangling skill representation into three explicit layers:

Scheduling — when to invoke
Structural — how it executes
Logical — what it does

This enables better skill discovery (+12% MRR) and risk assessment (+24% F1).
Alternative Approaches

Code-as-skill: Skills as executable functions with defined interfaces
Schema-based: JSON/YAML schemas with typed fields
Hybrid: Markdown for humans + structured metadata for machines
Scenario-mediated skill graph: Skills as directed edges between scenario nodes, enabling compositional path sampling for task synthesis (skillsynth)
Internalization: Train skills into model weights, eliminating inference-time retrieval (skill0), +9.7% on ALFWorld
Routing at scale: Full-text retrieve-and-rerank for selecting from 80K+ skill registries (skill-router), 74% Hit@1
Lifelong evolution: Discover → patch → maintain skills across sequential tasks (skillflow), +8.43 points
Skill marketplace: npm-like ecosystem for skills — creation, evaluation, and 200K+ curated repository (skillnet), +40% reward on ALFWorld/WebShop/ScienceWorld
Intrinsic evaluation: 5-dimension quality scoring (SCEMA) — assesses skill quality directly rather than via downstream proxy (skillnet)

Open Questions

Internalization vs. external library: skill0 shows skills can be embedded in weights; skill1 shows co-evolving a library works. When is each better?
Skill text quality matters: skill-router shows that full implementation bodies are critical for routing — representation quality directly impacts selection
Skill usage ≠ utility: skillflow reveals that creating skills is easier than extracting value from them

Related

ssl-representation — structured approach to this problem
skill-graph — graph-based skill organization with scenario-mediated transitions
skill-coevolution — joint optimization of skill selection, utilization, distillation
skillsynth — framework that uses skill graphs for training data synthesis
heuristic-system — software systems that also need skill-like modularity
coding-agents — the agents that consume skill representations
agent-memory — skills are stored as experiential memory; the Forms × Functions × Dynamics framework applies directly to skill storage

concepts/skill-routing.title: Skill Routing
concepts/skill-routing.content:
Skill Routing
The problem of selecting relevant skills from large skill registries (10K-100K+ entries) before downstream agent planning or execution. Introduced as a formal problem by skill-router.
Why It Matters
As skill ecosystems like clawhub grow, it becomes infeasible to expose every skill’s full text at inference time. Common practice of progressive disclosure (showing only skill names + descriptions, hiding implementation bodies) seems sensible but is empirically wrong at scale: hiding skill bodies causes a 31-44 percentage point drop in routing accuracy.
Key Finding

Full skill text is a critical routing signal, not minor metadata refinement.

The implementation body of a skill contains essential signals — tool affordances, edge cases, execution constraints — that names and descriptions alone cannot capture.
Metric

Hit@1: does the correct skill appear as the top-1 retrieval result?
skill-router achieves 74.0% Hit@1 on ~80K candidates with a 1.2B parameter pipeline

Design Space

























DimensionOptionsRetrievalSparse (BM25), dense (embeddings), hybridRerankingCross-encoder, LLM scoring, noneContext budgetFull body vs. progressive disclosureIndex freshnessStatic vs. continuously updated
Related Concepts

skill-router — the 1.2B pipeline that achieves SOTA routing accuracy
skill-graph — alternative to flat retrieval: skills as a traversable graph
skill-representation — the quality of skill text directly impacts routing
clawhub — real-world large skill registry that creates the routing problem
tiered-compilation — analogous scaling strategy for LLM wikis

concepts/srv6-source-routing.title: SRv6 Source Routing
concepts/srv6-source-routing.content:
SRv6 Source Routing
SRv6 (IPv6 Segment Routing) is a routing technique where the sender directly specifies the full path each packet should take through the network, rather than relying on switches to compute routes dynamically.
How It Works

Sender embeds the sequence of switch identifiers into each packet’s IPv6 destination address
Each switch checks if its own identifier is present in the address
If yes: the switch removes its identifier (shifts the address to reveal the next hop)
The switch looks up the next identifier in a static routing table (configured once, never changed)
Packet is forwarded to the next switch

In MRC
mrc uses SRv6 to eliminate dynamic routing entirely. Benefits:

Switches run simple, static routing tables — no BGP, no route recomputation
When a path fails, MRC simply stops using it — switches don’t need to adapt
Eliminates whole classes of dynamic routing failure modes
Enables detailed path-level statistics and congestion detection

Related

mrc — the protocol using SRv6
multi-plane-networks — the topology SRv6 routes across

concepts/ssl-representation.title: SSL Representation (Scheduling-Structural-Logical)
concepts/ssl-representation.content:
SSL Representation (Scheduling-Structural-Logical)
SSL (Scheduling-Structural-Logical) is a structured representation for agent skill artifacts, proposed by COOLPKU (2026). It is the first framework to disentangle the signals embedded in skill documents (like SKILL.md) into three explicit layers.
The Problem
LLM agent skills are currently represented as text-heavy artifacts (SKILL.md documents). This creates a representation problem:

Managing skill collections requires reasoning over invocation interfaces, execution structure, and side effects
These signals are entangled in a single textual surface
Machines can’t easily acquire or leverage the structured knowledge inside

Three Layers

























LayerWhat It CapturesExamplesSchedulingWhen to invoke this skill — triggers, preconditions, priority, inter-skill ordering”Run this when user asks for code review”, “Requires git repo initialized”StructuralHow the skill executes — control flow, scene decomposition, sub-steps, branchingSequence of steps, conditional branches, tool call orderingLogicalWhat the skill does — concrete actions, resource usage, side effects, tool invocations”Creates a file at ~/.hermes/…”, “Calls gh pr create”
Theoretical Foundation
SSL draws on Schank & Abelson’s classical cognitive linguistics:

Memory Organization Packets (MOPs) — organizing knowledge for recall and reuse
Script Theory — stereotypical sequences of events with slots and roles
Conceptual Dependency (CD) — primitive actions and state changes as semantic primitives

Implementation
An LLM-based normalizer converts raw SKILL.md documents into SSL-structured representations. The pipeline:
SKILL.md → LLM Normalizer → SSL Representation (3-layer structured output)

Evaluation Results























TaskBaseline (text-only)SSLImprovementSkill Discovery (MRR@50)0.6490.729+12.3%Risk Assessment (macro F1)0.4090.509+24.4%

Skill Discovery: Given a task description, rank relevant skills. SSL’s structured representation improves retrieval by separating scheduling signals from execution details.
Risk Assessment: Identify potential risks (security, resource, failure modes) in a skill. SSL’s explicit logical layer makes side effects and resource usage inspectable.

Implications
SSL positions itself as a practical step toward more inspectable, reusable, and operationally actionable skill representations — not a finished standard. The key insight: an explicit, source-grounded structure makes agent skills easier to search and review.
Relevance to Hermes Agent
Hermes Agent uses a SKILL.md-based skill system. The SSL framework could potentially:

Improve skill discovery (finding the right skill for a task)
Enable automated risk assessment of skills
Make skill execution more reliable through explicit structural representation

Related

coolpku — the research group behind SSL
schank-abelson-cognitive-linguistics — theoretical foundation
skill-representation — the broader problem SSL addresses
coding-agents — consumer of structured skill representations

concepts/terminal-agents.title: Terminal Agents
concepts/terminal-agents.content:
Terminal Agents
LLM-based agents that use the command-line interface (CLI) as a universal action space, enabling them to execute complex, long-horizon tasks across computing systems. A subset of coding-agents.
How They Work
At each step t, a terminal agent π observes the terminal state oₜ and samples an action aₜ ~ π(· | o≤ₜ, a<ₜ, g), where g is a natural-language goal. The low-level trajectory ζ = (o₀, a₀, …, o_T) is successful if the final state satisfies an external verifier.
Scenario–Skill Abstraction
Low-level trajectories are lifted to a higher semantic abstraction via two objects:

Scenario σₜ: A decision-relevant abstraction of the observation at an execution decision point. Serves as a sufficient statistic of interaction history.
Skill κₜ: An action subsequence applied at one scenario that produces a predictable transition to the next: κₜ : σₜ₋₁ → σₜ.

An execution trajectory becomes: ξ = (σ₀, κ₁, σ₁, κ₂, …, κ_L, σ_L)
This formulation links to options in hierarchical RL (Sutton et al., 1999).
Training Bottleneck
Terminal agents’ capabilities are fundamentally limited by the scarcity of high-quality, diverse execution trajectories. The learnable region of the agent’s policy is confined to the support of the training distribution — scenarios with p(σ|g)=0 are unobservable, skills with p(κ|σ,g)=0 are unexercised. Maximizing learned capacity requires training data that densely covers the conditional product space {(σ, κ)}.
Key Systems

skillsynth — Synthesizes training tasks by sampling paths from a skill-graph
Terminus 2 — Agent scaffold interacting solely through a headless terminal
Harbor — Orchestration framework for parallelized evaluation across 128 Docker environments
Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Junie CLI — Proprietary terminal agent scaffolds

Evaluation

terminal-bench 1.0 and 2.0 — community-curated benchmarks (80 and 89 tasks respectively)
Top proprietary models: GPT-5.3-Codex (64.7% TB 2.0), Claude Opus 4.6 (62.9%)

Common Failure Modes

































ModeRatioPartial implementation (skipping clauses)42.2%Over-trust in inline self-tests29.5%Premature termination12.9%API/flag hallucination7.0%Debug fixation loop5.1%Error rationalization3.3%
Related Concepts

coding-agents — broader category of LLM coding agents
skill-graph — structured representation of skills and their transition scenarios
skillsynth — task synthesis framework for terminal agents
skill-representation — the problem of encoding reusable agent capabilities

concepts/tiered-compilation.title: Tiered Compilation
concepts/tiered-compilation.content:
Tiered Compilation
A scaling strategy for LLM-powered wiki compilers that routes source documents through different processing tiers based on file type, usage patterns, and user demand. Introduced by sage-wiki to handle vaults of 10K-100K+ documents.
The Four Tiers








































TierNameWhat HappensCost per DocLatency0Index onlyFTS5 full-text search indexFree~5ms1Index + embedFTS5 + vector embedding~$0.00002~200ms2Code parseStructural summary via regex (no LLM)Free~10ms3Full compileSummarize + extract concepts + write articles~$0.05-0.15~5-8 min
Design Principles

Default fast, compile on demand — set default_tier: 1 to index everything quickly (~5.5 hours for 100K docs), then compile specific topics when queried
Auto-promotion — sources that get 3+ search hits or reach 5+ in a topic cluster auto-promote to Tier 3
Auto-demotion — stale articles (90 days without queries) demote to Tier 1
File-type defaults — JSON, YAML, lock files skip to Tier 0; code files use Tier 2 parsers
Adaptive backpressure — concurrency self-tunes to provider rate limits

Code Parsers (Tier 2)
10 built-in parsers: Go (via go/ast), TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Rust, Java, C, C++, Ruby, plus JSON/YAML/TOML key extraction. Code files get structural summaries without LLM calls, enabling free indexing of large codebases.
Compile-on-Demand
When an agent queries a topic via MCP (wiki_compile_topic), search results signal which sources are uncompiled. The compiler then processes just that topic cluster (~2 min for 20 sources), rather than recompiling the entire vault.
Related Concepts

sage-wiki — the tool that implements tiered compilation
output-trust — verification for the answers produced by compiled knowledge
catastrophic-forgetting — analogous problem in neural networks: tiered compilation avoids “catastrophic recompilation” of entire vaults

concepts/white-box-transformers.title: White-Box Transformers
concepts/white-box-transformers.content:
White-Box Transformers
An approach to neural network architecture design introduced in deep-representation-learning-book (Chapter 5) where each layer has an explicit mathematical interpretation derived from optimization of the rate-reduction objective.
Core Idea

Deep networks are not black boxes — they are unrolled optimization algorithms whose layers correspond to steps of an iterative optimization process.

Instead of empirically designing architectures through trial-and-error (the “alchemy” approach), white-box networks are derived by:

Writing down the optimization objective (ΔR maximization)
Choosing an iterative optimization algorithm (gradient descent, ISTA, LISTA)
Unrolling the iterations into network layers
Each layer = one optimization step

How Attention Emerges
The key breakthrough: Transformer attention is not an arbitrary design choice — it emerges naturally from gradient steps on the sparse rate reduction objective:

































Optimization ConceptNetwork ComponentGradient of coding rate w.r.t. ZQuery-Key computationSparsity-promoting projectionSoftmax normalizationValue aggregationWeighted feature combinationIncremental update (Rₜ₊₁ = Rₜ + η·∇ΔR)Residual connectionMulti-step unrollingMulti-head attentionIterative shrinkage (ISTA)Feed-forward layers
From Black-Box to White-Box





























Black-Box DesignWhite-Box Design”Let’s try 12 layers""12 optimization steps needed""Attention works, add more heads""Sparsity pattern requires this many components""Skip connections help""Incremental gradient updates”Architecture search (NAS)Derivation from objectiveAblation-drivenPrinciple-driven
Connection to Existing Architectures
The white-box framework explains why certain architectures work:

ResNet: Skip connections ≡ incremental gradient updates
DenseNet: Dense connections ≡ accumulated gradient history (momentum-like)
ViT: Patch-based attention ≡ block-sparse rate reduction
MoE: Expert routing ≡ structured sparsity in optimization

Practical Implications

Interpretability: Every layer’s function is mathematically defined — you know why each component exists
Efficiency: Architecture can be sized according to problem complexity rather than guesswork
Transferability: The same optimization principle applies across modalities (vision, language, speech)
Verifiability: You can check whether the network is actually performing the intended optimization

Limitations
The book acknowledges that current white-box designs are still approximations. The unrolling from sparse ΔR to Transformer attention involves relaxations and approximations. Full mathematical equivalence remains an open problem.
Key Source
deep-representation-learning-book, Chapter 5: “Deep Representations as Unrolled Optimization.”
Cross-References

deep-representation-learning — The paradigm this serves
rate-reduction — The objective being optimized
closed-loop-transcription — Where white-box encoders are deployed
compression-representation — The compression perspective
yi-ma — Primary architect

concepts/workflow-generation.title: Workflow Generation with LLMs
concepts/workflow-generation.content:
Workflow Generation with LLMs

大模型生成、编排和优化多步骤工作流的能力。涵盖从自然语言描述到可执行 DAG 的完整 pipeline。

问题定义
LLM Workflow Generation 的核心挑战：给定一个用自然语言描述的高层目标，模型需要生成一组有序、可执行的子任务（actions/nodes），并正确处理它们之间的依赖关系（DAG 拓扑）。这比单纯的文字生成或单步工具调用困难得多：

结构约束：生成的子任务必须形成合法的有向无环图（DAG），节点之间不能有循环依赖
动作可行性：每个节点必须对应真实可调用的 API/工具，参数类型必须匹配
可执行性：最终生成的工作流必须能在目标平台（Dify、Coze、Apple Shortcuts 等）上实际运行
效率优化：在满足正确性的前提下，最大化并行度、最小化总延迟

主要技术路线
1. 搜索式：将 Workflow 优化建模为搜索问题
代表工作：aflow（ICLR 2025）
AFlow 的核心创新是将 workflow optimization 视为搜索问题，使用 MCTS（蒙特卡洛树搜索）
自动发现最优 agentic workflow。它提供一组 operators（Generate, Review, Revise, Ensemble, Test, Programmer），
每个 operator 由 LLM 实现，通过执行反馈评价候选 workflow。

核心思想：手工设计 workflow 成本高、不通用 → 用搜索自动发现最优结构

2. 知识图谱增强：利用领域 KG 指导 Workflow 生成
代表工作：opus（arXiv 2024）、knowagent（NAACL 2025 Findings）
Opus 引入 Work Knowledge Graph (WKG) 显式编码业务领域知识：

将客户输入→目标输出→业务上下文组织为 workflow intention
从 WKG 中检索相关流程知识指导 Large Work Model 生成候选 workflow
多候选合成为 Workflow Graph，按成本/时间/质量约束做路径优化

KnowAgent 则构造 action knowledge base，告诉模型哪些动作的前置/后置条件关系，
从而在生成 Thought-Action-Observation 轨迹时减少 invalid action 和 misordered action。

核心思想：通用 LLM 缺乏领域-specific 的流程知识 → KG 提供结构化先验

3. 编译式：LLM 生成 DAG，调度器并行执行
代表工作：llmcompiler（ICML 2024）
LLMCompiler 打破传统 ReAct 的串行瓶颈：

模型先生成 function-calling task DAG，标识任务间依赖
调度器并行运行无依赖的函数调用
显著降低延迟和成本，部分任务准确率也提升

与 AFlow 的区别：LLMCompiler 的 DAG 由 LLM 直接生成（非搜索），重点是并行调度。

核心思想：ReAct 的”思考→行动→观察”循环造成不必要的串行 → 先规划 DAG 再并行执行

4. 翻译-规划：LLM 翻译问题 + 经典规划器求解
代表工作：llm-p（arXiv 2023）
LLM+P 采用分工策略：

LLM 负责将自然语言问题翻译为 PDDL 形式化问题
Classical Planner 在形式化状态空间中搜索最优计划
LLM 再将形式化计划翻译回自然语言


核心思想：LLM 擅长语言理解但不擅长可靠搜索 → 把规划计算交给专业规划器

5. 微调式：用大规模数据训练模型学会 Workflow 编排
代表工作：workflowllm（arXiv 2024）
WorkflowLLM 不依赖外部规划器或 KG：

从 Apple Shortcuts + RoutineHub 收集大规模真实 workflow 数据 → WorkflowBench 数据集
将 workflow 转写为 Python 风格代码，补充任务描述、计划和 API 信息
微调模型，使模型内化 workflow 编排能力


核心思想：通用模型未见过真实 workflow 的复杂性 → 用真实数据微调

评测维度



































维度关注点代表 Benchmark拓扑正确性DAG 依赖关系是否合法worfbench (ICLR 2025)可执行性能否在平台上实际运行chat2workflow (arXiv 2026)动作准确性调用的 API/工具是否正确WorkflowBench (WorkflowLLM)任务完成率最终是否满足用户需求Chat2Workflow 的端到端测试效率/并行度延迟、成本、并行利用率LLMCompiler 的 ParallelQA
开放问题

跨平台泛化：当前方法多针对特定平台（Shortcuts/Dify/Coze），缺乏统一的 workflow DSL 标准
动态适应：Workflow 执行中如何根据中间结果调整后续步骤（closed-loop）
复杂约束：如何表达和满足时间、成本、安全等多维约束
人机协作：如何让人类在 workflow generation 过程中提供反馈和修正
长程依赖：Action 序列超过 50+ 步时，当前方法正确率急剧下降

相关概念

dag-planning-in-llm — DAG 规划方法的深入分析
tool-use-in-llm — LLM 工具调用的基础技术
workflow-llm-comparison — 9 篇关键论文的系统对比

entities/agent结构化记忆方案解读hindsight细分记忆及gra.content:
今天是2026年4月7日 ，星期二，北京，天气晴
继续看Agent记忆进展 ，昨天看10种Agent记忆方案对比总结，顺着这个思路，我们继续看看结构化记忆这条路怎么做？
解读两个工作，一个是基于细分结构化及时序的Agent记忆思路 HINDSIGHT，重点看看记忆的构建和检索思路，找些思路，尤其是做的这个路由分类、知识图谱的构建策略、结合时序上的检索机制
另一个是面向AI编码助手的知识图谱构建插件graphify，看看具体怎么做的，虽然很粗暴。
关注细节，关注底层逻辑，会有收获 。
一、细分结构化及时序的Agent记忆思路HINDSIGHT
来看一个有趣的工作，基于细分结构化记忆的Agent记忆思路 ，做的结构化长期记忆架构 ，核心就是细分了个网络记忆结构 （世界、经验、观点、观察，也就是world, experience, opinion, observation，然后将每个提取的事实做路由分类到这几个里面，所以可以理解是细化 ），工作在《HINDSIGHT IS 20/20: BUILDING AGENT 》， 地址在：hindsight.vectorize.io，github.com/vectorize-io/hindsight，报告地址在：arxiv.org/pdf/2512.12818，github.com/vectorize-io/hindsight/blob/main/hindsight-docs/static/img/retain-operation.webp，
重点看看记忆的构建和检索思路，找些思路，尤其是做的这个路由分类、这个时序上的检索机制
1、记忆的构建方式
这个操作会用大模型从输入中提取关键事实、时间信息、实体和关系 ；再通过标准化流程，把提取的数据转化为规范实体、时间序列、检索索引+元数据 ；这些结构化表示，会为后续Recall（回忆/检索）和Reflect（反思/推理）操作，做索引通路 。
这个流程也不闲再啰嗦，可以再看下：
RetainInput ，做记忆留存，输入可以是用户对话、文档、日志、多模态信息等原始数据；
step1.抽取
LLMFactExtraction ，用大模型对原始输入做结构化抽取，生成包含每个事实的叙述性文本、规范化的时间信息（包括时间范围）、参与者及其角色、指示目标网络的事实类型：
其中，将相对时间引用（如“上周”、“三月”）转换为绝对时间戳 (τs, τe)；
事实类型分类，根据陈述的性质分配 {world, experience, opinion, observation}，这很粗暴 。
step2：对齐、消歧
Entity Recognition & Resolution，实体识别 + 消歧，从事实中定位实体，并解决同名、别名问题，统一实体指代。
其中，实体抽取，识别PERSON、ORGANIZATION、LOCATION、PRODUCT、CONCEPT和 OTHER 等实体类型 。
消歧方面，通过结合字符串和名称相似度（例如，Levenshtein 距离）、与其他实体的共现模式以及提及的时间接近性，通过最大化相似度得分，这个很常用 。
Normalized Canonical Entities ，输出标准化的规范实体。
step3：建立关系边
包括两步：
一个是Create Memory Units（创建记忆单元 ），把前面的事实、实体、时间信息，封装成最小记忆单元，每个单元包含text 【原始/摘要化的事实文本】、embeddings 【文本的向量嵌入（用于语义检索）】、timestamps 【时间戳（用于时间维度检索）】、metadata 【元数据（如来源、事实类型、置信度等）】；
一个是Link Construction（关联构建） ，给记忆单元之间建立多维度关联，让记忆从孤立条目变成知识图谱，包括；
Entity Links 做实体关联、Temporal Links做时间关联，时间先后关系。
Semantic Links 【语义关联，比如AI算法和大模型、知识图谱的语义相似/从属关系】；
Causal Links 【因果关联，比如用户学习Agent技术→用户关注OpenClaw项目的因果关系】；
step4:记忆路由存储
抽取好后，存储，按记忆的类型，把记忆单元分发到不同的存储分区 ，做分类管理【为了效率，每个路由类型对应一个独立的存储分区，方便按类型快速检索】，具体的包括多个，World世界知识/客观事实+Experience-个人/ Agent的经历、交互历史+Opinion-观点、偏好、态度+Observation-观察、感知到的信息 。
2、记忆检索方式
检索方式还是很常规的Multi-Strategy Retrieval (TEMPR)，这种典型的多路召回策略，RAG中常用，也可以回顾下RAG的做法。
语义检索 ，用于概念相似性、同义转述场景；
关键词检索（BM25） ，用于人名、专业术语、精确匹配场景；
图谱检索 ，用于关联实体、间接关联推理场景；
时序检索 ，用于“去年春天”“六月”、时间范围类查询，这个可以展开看看【会用到】
当查询中检测到时间约束时 ，调用一个由混合时间解析器支持的时间图检索，具体的首先运行一个基于规则的分析器 ，使用两个现成的多语言日期解析库，将显式和相对表达式（例如，“昨天”、“上个周末”或“2024年6月”）归一化为一个日期范围 。
这种启发式路径以低延迟处理了大部分查询。对于无法通过启发式方法解决的查询，回退到一个轻量级的序列到序列模型（此处使用google/~an-t5-small），将剩余的时间表达式转换为具体的日期范围[τstart,τend]。随后，将其与记忆的出现区间进行匹配。
最后，采用 cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 模型，模型会联合编码查询和每个候选记忆项，并输出相关性得分。
二、面向AI编码助手的知识图谱构建插件graphify
来看一个工作，面向AI编码助手的知识图谱构建技能，也可以叫做插件，其打的点还是，基于图的存储，降低token消耗 。
工作在：github.com/safishamsi/graphify/blob/v3/README.md，这类工具其实很多了，zep、graphiti等有好多，但是再熟悉下也行，看看怎么做，有哪些组件，最终成型是啥。
其slogan是：在ClaudeCode、Codex、OpenCode或OpenClaw中输入/graphify，读取你的文件、构建知识图谱，口号是直接丢进去代码、PDF、Markdown、截图、流程图，用Claudevision从这些内容中提取概念和关系，然后搞成图谱。
1、工作原理
因为面向的数据不一样，所以需要采用不同的处理机制，如下：
对于**.py .ts .js .go .rs .java .c .cpp .rb .cs .kt .scala .php .swift .lua格式的代码（Code），通过 tree-sitter生成抽象语法树（AST）+调用关系图+文档字符串/注释中的设计逻辑，这是一款开源的语法解析工具，可生成 语言代码的抽象语法树 （AST，代码语法结构的树形表示，用于精准提取类、函数、call-graph（调用关系图，记录代码中函数/方法之间调用关系的图结构，用于梳理代码执行链路）、docstring/commentrationale（代码中的文档字符串和注释，用于提取代码的设计思路、实现逻辑代码结构信息）这个是做 code graph，以及做chunking的常用操作 ，代码在：github.com/safishamsi/graphify/blob/v3/graphify/extract.py
对于.md，.txt，.rst格式的文档（Docs） ，通过Claude提取概念、关联关系与设计逻辑；
对于pdf格式的论文（Papers） ，进行citation mining （引用关系挖掘，从论文中提取参考文献、引用链路，梳理学术脉络）+概念提取；对于.png，.jpg，.webp，.gif格式的图像，使用Claude视觉能力做提取，可识别图像中的文字、图表、截图等内容。
然后，提取其中的概念、关联关系与设计逻辑的细节 是，所有关联关系均标注成三个类型，包括EXTRACTED （提取，直接从源码中获取）、INFERRED（推断，合理推导结果，附带置信度评分）、AMBIGUOUS （歧义，标记待人工复核），这里的设计逻辑是：可清晰区分哪些信息是直接提取所得，哪些为推导猜测 。
然后，上述结果会合并为一张NetworkX图 ，通过莱顿社区发现算法 进行聚类，最终输出为交互式HTML文件、可查询的JSON数据以及review报告。
2、一个例子
例如给定数据为：github.com/safishamsi/graphify/tree/v3/worked/mixed-corpus/raw
处理后，得到如下结果：
其中：
graph.json(github.com/safishamsi/graphify/blob/v3/worked/mixed-corpus/graph.json)为对应的graph抽取结果，其实并不细节：
review.md(github.com/safishamsi/graphify/blob/v3/worked/mixed-corpus/review.md)为整个的实现过程：
pipeline的过程是Pipeline: detect → extract (AST) → build → cluster → analyze → query → feedback loop
GRAPH_REPORT.md （github.com/safishamsi/graphify/blob/v3/worked/mixed-corpus/GRAPH_REPORT.md），主要生成的是对这个graph的分析：
参考文献
1、github.com/safishamsi/graphify/blob/v3/README.md
2、arxiv.org/pdf/2512.12818
关于我们
老刘，主页：liuhuanyong.github.io。

entities/agent记忆框架详解tencentdb-agent-memo.slug: entities/agent记忆框架详解tencentdb-agent-memory的分层式长期记忆及符号化短期记忆设-1
entities/agent记忆框架详解tencentdb-agent-memo.filePath: entities/Agent记忆框架详解TencentDB-Agent-Memory的分层式长期记忆及符号化短期记忆设-1.md
entities/agent记忆框架详解tencentdb-agent-memo.title: Agent记忆框架详解：TencentDB Agent Memory的分层式长期记忆及符号化短期记忆设计细节
entities/agent记忆框架详解tencentdb-agent-memo.content:
今天是2026年5月24日 ，星期日，北京，天气晴
来看Agent记忆方面的进展，我们之前已经讲过很多了，尤其是基于图的Agent记忆、基于树的、基于层次的记忆方案等 ，都在解决上下文超载问题、个性化问题 。
最近有个方案很火，TencentDB-Agent-Memory，基于上下文卸载与结构化图表示的短期记忆压缩方案 ，通过将完整信息卸载至外部文件系统，同时利用 Mermaid将任务执行过程转化为可导航的结构化记忆 ，这个Mermaid的方案有意思，所以展开来看看。
官方的地址在《TencentDB Agent Memory 》(github.com/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory)，对应的解读在：deepwiki.com/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory，zread.ai/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory，官方也有一些解读，在：mp.weixin.qq.com/s/mK5WoNgJSPSpdmsUSSDqPw，又如：mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg，解读基于此开展。
先说具体结论：
其一，TencentDB Agent Memory=符号化短期记忆+分层式长期记忆 ，也就是用于渐进式分层长期记忆，以及用于任务内上下文压缩的符号化短期记忆，分开处理。
其二，在实际处理时候，这些各层也是互相协同的。分成两个阶段：
提示词构建前（handleBeforeRecall）阶段 ：召回流程搜索L1记忆（通过混合向量+关键词搜索），加载L3人设，并组装场景导航。它们作为prependContext（每轮的L1记忆）和appendSystemContext（稳定的人设+场景导航+工具指南）被注入到提示词中。
轮次结束后（handleTurnCommitted）阶段 ：将新消息写入L0，当累积了足够的新消息时触发L1提取，并根据各自的触发条件调度L2场景聚合和L3人设生成，与此同时，卸载流水线将工具结果压缩为Mermaid符号。
与此对应的，就是是目前很流行的渐进式披露，Agent 在上下文中关注紧凑的高层结构（人设、场景导航、Mermaid 画布），仅在需要时向下钻取至原始证据。长期记忆上，钻取路径始终是确定性的—从一个人设特征，到促成它的场景，到这些场景内的 L1 记忆，再到产生这些记忆的 L0 消息；而对于短期任务，则是从一个 Mermaid node_id 到完整的卸载日志 。
基于这些，本文分别看分层式长期记忆和符号化符号化短期记忆具体怎么做的，里面的mermaid是怎么做的，并最后做个总结。
一、TencentDB Agent Memory的分层式长期记忆
长期记忆指的是历时对话，底层是原始对话，顶层是提炼后的用户画像。每一层对下层进行提炼与压缩 ，Agent在推理时只需关注顶层的抽象即可，当需要细节时，可以沿着确定性的溯源链向下钻取。
因此，实现方式是建立L0 Conversation（原始对话）→L1 Atom（结构化事实）→L2 Scenario（场景块）→L3 Persona（用户画像） 的层级结构，平时靠高层Persona把握用户偏好，需要考证细节时再检索底层。
实现流程也是一个流水线：从L0对话捕获→L1提取与去重→L2场景聚合→L3人设生成 。
可以细分看下：
1、L0 Conversation（原始对话）
将每条用户和助手的消息原样记录到每日的 JSONL 文件中（conversations/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl）。具体实现上，不进行摘要、不过滤，只是忠实地捕获，每条消息都携带着唯一的 id、sessionKey 和时间戳 。
2、L1 Atom（结构化事实） ，在L0的基础上，使用LLM提取出原子记忆
每条记忆被划分为三种类型之一，并被赋予一个优先级分数。
特别的，提取之后使用批量去重步骤，使用向量相似度召回，将新记忆与已有记忆进行比较，去重引擎为每条记忆决定以下四种操作之一：store（新事实）、update（替换过时事实）、merge（合并重叠事实）或 skip（已知事实） ，这能防止存储空间因冗余条目而无限制地增长。
结果被持久化到两个互补的存储中：仅追加的 JSONL 文件 （records/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl）作为持久可靠的数据源，以及向量数据库（SQLite + sqlite-vec 或 Tencent Cloud VectorDB ）作为主检索引擎。每条记忆记录都带有 source_message_ids，将其链接回生成它的 L0 消息。
这一步完整的prompt在：
3、L2 Scenario（场景块） ，这一步的每个场景块都是一个Markdown 文件
包含一个META分隔头部，其后是叙事内容。META部分携带索引元数据——创建时间戳、最后更新时间戳、简洁的摘要和热度分数 。
具体实现上，使用一个带有文件工具的 LLM Agent-SceneExtractor，自主读取现有的场景文件(文件工具（read、write、edit）自主读写 .md 文件)，决定是CREATE（创建）一个新场景、UPDATE（更新）现有场景，还是 MERGE（合并）相似场景——然后直接写入 Markdown 文件 。当场景数量接近配置的最大值（默认：15）时，LLM被指示在创建前先进行合并，以保持场景空间的可用性。
对应的prmopt如下：
不过，这个有个很设计要点，在scene_blocks/ 的工作区沙箱内运行 。
4、L3 Persona（用户画像），所有场景中提炼出用户稳定的特征、工作风格和偏好
该文件在每一轮都会被注入到系统提示词中，也是一个md文件 。
具体实现上，与场景提取类似，人设生成使用一个带有工具的LLMAgent，它读取场景块并直接写入persona.md 。
对应的prompt(src/core/prompts/persona-generation.ts)如下：
从中看，是一个结构化的分析框架 ，指导 LLM通过四个逐渐深入的层次扫描场景数据，约束LLM的分析以产生可操作的、基于证据的结果。
二、短期记忆（上下文卸载/任务）
这里的短期记忆是在漫长的任务会话中，冗长的工具输出（搜索结果、代码清单、错误追踪）消耗了上下文窗口的绝大部分，所以，解决方式，就是上下文卸载和任务分层 ，通过一套独立于长期记忆管道的三阶段压缩流水线来实现 ，分成底层markdown文件、中层json条目、顶层mermaid流程图 三个层次。
在具体执行时，Agent在上下文中仅需关注顶层结构，遇错时再沿着node_id下钻到底层查证。
这里又可以进一步拆分出来L1.5如下：
具体的：
Step1. L1 ：工具输出 → 摘要 OffloadEntry + 原文存入 refs/*.md;
Step2. L1.5 ：判定长任务边界，激活 MMD 文件;
Step3. L2 ：后台生成/增量更新 Mermaid 流程图;
Step4. 注入器 ：把紧凑的 Mermaid 图注入上下文，替代冗长的工具历史;
Step5. L3 回收 ：当上下文继续膨胀时，轻度 ：用摘要替换工具结果（原文在 refs/ 中无损保留）；激进 ：删除最早的消息，但注入历史 MMD 图作为补偿（mmd-injector.ts#L294-L301）； 紧急 ：更激进截断.
这里的关键在于：每一步压缩都有回溯路径 。从 MMD 的 node_id → offload.jsonl 中的条目 → refs/*.md 中的完整原文。压缩不是失忆，而是渐进式披露。
这里要展开去讲几个点。
1、Mermaid流程图做任务图的逻辑
短期上下文卸载采用Mermaid流程图作为符号压缩层，这一步解决的是将庞大的工具调用历史转化为紧凑、语义丰富的任务图 。
关于这块，文章（mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg ）做了个较好的解读， Mermaid有几个很适合 Agent 的特点，如下：可以表达节点、边、状态、依赖关系，节点可以绑定 node_id、summary、result_ref。
此外，重点尝试了两种格式：Flowchart 和 StateDiagram ，结论就是Flowchart 相比 StateDiagram 效果约提升 15%，在长任务上下文卸载场景中，Agent 更需要一张表达复杂依赖和多路径探索且足够自由的任务地图，StateDiagram 更适合描述有严格生命周期的对象，Flowchart 更适合描述 Agent 高度自由的探索式执行过程。
2、Mermaid流程图做任务图的具体实现
但这个仅仅是结构，还是要具体承载，在具体实现上，对应的promp t如下：
调用本地LLM，输入三样东西：现有 MMD 内容（带行号注释，方便精确引用）、新的 OffloadEntry 列表、近期对话历史，
LLM输出Mermaid图中每个节点有如下结构，如：001-N1["summary: 查询数据库状态<br/>status: done"] 001-N2["summary: 执行迁移脚本<br/>status: doing"]，node_id（如 001-N1）：唯一标识，可映射回 refs/*.md 中的原始工具输出，status：done / doing / todo，告诉 Agent 任务进展，MMD 文件头部还有  元数据 。
在形式化上，底层保留原始、厚重的工具调用结果（refs/*.md），中层抽取步骤摘要（jsonl），高层则浓缩为一张极度轻量的Mermaid任务画布。每个Mermaid图表均以.mmd文件的形式持久化存储在Agent的mmds/目录下，文件遵循严格的结构：
由分隔符包围的JSON元数据头部，其后是flowchartTD主体，主体中的节点均带有结构化标签。
其中，JSON元数据头部 编码了任务的身份标识与进度，对应【严格时间戳与元数据规则】：
1)顶部元数据（必填） ：（updatedTime为node中的最新时间）。
2)节点内时间 ：如果合并了多个新条目，节点内的 Timestamp 必须取其中最新的 ISO 时间。
此外也有【严格的工程底线】 ，如下：
1)节点标准格式 ：NodeID["阶段名: 宏观动作简述<br/>status: done|doing|paused|blocked <br/>summary: 核心结论摘要<br/>Timestamp: ISO8601"]
对应4种状态：
其中，blocked 状态是系统的防重复机制：通过将死胡同显式记录为墓碑节点，LLM 避免了浪费 token 去重新尝试已经失败的方法，L2 提示词将其称为”认知墓碑” (cognitive tombstones)。
2)全员归宿映射 ：输入的每一个新 tool_call_id，都必须在 node_mapping 中被分配到一个 Node ID；MMD里的每一个node都应该有源头的tool_call消息来源，不能乱编，绝对不允许遗漏 （Node_id和tool_call_id是一对多的关系）
3、mermaid的注入形式
当然，生成Mermaid图表还不够，图表必须被注入到 LLM 的消息数组中的正确位置，才能能充当压缩上下文，具体插入到上下文的原则是：
找到最新的user消息位置，如果它在对话后半段→插入到它之后，如果在前半段→找到尾部工具循环块的起始位置（距尾部≤30条），关键保护：不会拆散assistant(tool_use)→tool_result的配对，这样MMD恰好坐在”用户问题”和”进行中的工具循环”之间，Agent先看到任务概览图，再看具体工具操作 。
最终注入到上下文中的是一条 user 消息，结构如下：
4、mermaid画布的执行意义
因此，在这个mermaid画布搭建完之后，可以看具体用法，这里援引mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg 的论述：

第一层是鸟瞰/Overview 。Agent先看到的是任务级别的概览：之前做过哪些任务，哪些已经完成，哪些还在进行，当前用户的问题可能和哪一个历史任务有关。这一层不追求细节，而是帮助Agent快速判断方向；
第二层是聚焦 。当Agent判断某个任务相关时，它再打开对应的任务画布，查看这个任务内部的结构。此时，Agent看到的不是一长串对话历史，而是一张经过组织的任务地图：哪些步骤已经完成，哪些节点还在进行，哪些信息汇聚成了当前结论；
第三层是下钻/细节补充 。当画布上的节点信息仍然不够时，Agent才进一步追溯到更细的摘要或原始材料。也就是说，原文不是一直常驻上下文，而是在真正需要确认细节时才被重新展开。

三、最后的总结-优劣势
总的来说，TencentDBAgentMemory是一个面向AIAgent的分层记忆系统，核心解决两个问题：
长期记忆方面 ，通过跨会话记住用户偏好、项目上下文、SOP，通过L0→L1→L2→L3的渐进式蒸馏，每层由LLM驱动提取；
短期记忆方面 ，单次长任务中上下文窗口不够用，通过Mermaid符号压缩+外部文件卸载+三级回收机制，每一层压缩都保留确定性回溯路径（node_id→refs/*.md），区别于粗暴摘要，这种设计让Agent在上下文中只保留高层结构（人设、场景导航、Mermaid画布），需要细节时下钻。比扁平向量库更合适些。
其中有两个有趣的设计。一个是回溯性 ，这是核心保障，这意味着压缩不是有损摘要，是可逆的。此外，用流程图替代冗长工具日志，token占用降一个数量级，同时Mermaid是LLM天然能理解的结构化格式 ，增量更新机制（指纹比对），避免了每次全量重写。
但是，也有一些不足，也看下：
其一，配置复杂度过高，光是OffloadConfig就有20+个参数，真正用好这套系统需要理解大量调优参数 （l2NullThreshold、mmdMaxTokenRatio、aggressiveCompressRatio……），对普通用户门槛很高。
其二，LLM调用成本和延迟不可忽视，每个管道阶段 （L1提取、L1.5边界判定、L2Mermaid生成、L2场景聚合、L3人设生成）都需要LLM调用。一次长任务会话可能触发数十次LLM调用。延迟和成本是真实存在的，尤其是在高并发场景下。
其三，L2生成的Mermaid图完全依赖LLM输出 。如果LLM产生格式错误、node_id冲突、或者语义遗漏，系统虽然有backfillNodeIds做回填，但没有对Mermaid严格语义正确性的校验，可能出出错，导致Agent不稳定。
所以，适合自己的，才是最好的，东西设计的越复杂，其实不可控因素越多 。
参考文献
1、mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg
2、github.com/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory

基于这些，本文分别看分层式长期记忆和符号化符号化短期记忆具体怎么做的，里面的mermaid是怎么做的，并最后做个总结。
一、TencentDB Agent Memory的分层式长期记忆
长期记忆指的是历时对话，底层是原始对话，顶层是提炼后的用户画像。每一层对下层进行提炼与压缩 ，Agent在推理时只需关注顶层的抽象即可，当需要细节时，可以沿着确定性的溯源链向下钻取。
因此，实现方式是建立L0 Conversation（原始对话）→L1 Atom（结构化事实）→L2 Scenario（场景块）→L3 Persona（用户画像） 的层级结构，平时靠高层Persona把握用户偏好，需要考证细节时再检索底层。
实现流程也是一个流水线：从L0对话捕获→L1提取与去重→L2场景聚合→L3人设生成 。
可以细分看下：
1、L0 Conversation（原始对话）
将每条用户和助手的消息原样记录到每日的 JSONL 文件中（conversations/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl）。具体实现上，不进行摘要、不过滤，只是忠实地捕获，每条消息都携带着唯一的 id、sessionKey 和时间戳 。
2、L1 Atom（结构化事实） ，在L0的基础上，使用LLM提取出原子记忆
每条记忆被划分为三种类型之一，并被赋予一个优先级分数。
特别的，提取之后使用批量去重步骤，使用向量相似度召回，将新记忆与已有记忆进行比较，去重引擎为每条记忆决定以下四种操作之一：store（新事实）、update（替换过时事实）、merge（合并重叠事实）或 skip（已知事实） ，这能防止存储空间因冗余条目而无限制地增长。
结果被持久化到两个互补的存储中：仅追加的 JSONL 文件 （records/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl）作为持久可靠的数据源，以及向量数据库（SQLite + sqlite-vec 或 Tencent Cloud VectorDB ）作为主检索引擎。每条记忆记录都带有 source_message_ids，将其链接回生成它的 L0 消息。
这一步完整的prompt在：
3、L2 Scenario（场景块） ，这一步的每个场景块都是一个Markdown 文件
包含一个META分隔头部，其后是叙事内容。META部分携带索引元数据——创建时间戳、最后更新时间戳、简洁的摘要和热度分数 。
具体实现上，使用一个带有文件工具的 LLM Agent-SceneExtractor，自主读取现有的场景文件(文件工具（read、write、edit）自主读写 .md 文件)，决定是CREATE（创建）一个新场景、UPDATE（更新）现有场景，还是 MERGE（合并）相似场景——然后直接写入 Markdown 文件 。当场景数量接近配置的最大值（默认：15）时，LLM被指示在创建前先进行合并，以保持场景空间的可用性。
对应的prmopt如下：
不过，这个有个很设计要点，在scene_blocks/ 的工作区沙箱内运行 。
4、L3 Persona（用户画像），所有场景中提炼出用户稳定的特征、工作风格和偏好
该文件在每一轮都会被注入到系统提示词中，也是一个md文件 。
具体实现上，与场景提取类似，人设生成使用一个带有工具的LLMAgent，它读取场景块并直接写入persona.md 。
对应的prompt(src/core/prompts/persona-generation.ts)如下：
从中看，是一个结构化的分析框架 ，指导 LLM通过四个逐渐深入的层次扫描场景数据，约束LLM的分析以产生可操作的、基于证据的结果。
二、短期记忆（上下文卸载/任务）
这里的短期记忆是在漫长的任务会话中，冗长的工具输出（搜索结果、代码清单、错误追踪）消耗了上下文窗口的绝大部分，所以，解决方式，就是上下文卸载和任务分层 ，通过一套独立于长期记忆管道的三阶段压缩流水线来实现 ，分成底层markdown文件、中层json条目、顶层mermaid流程图 三个层次。
在具体执行时，Agent在上下文中仅需关注顶层结构，遇错时再沿着node_id下钻到底层查证。
这里又可以进一步拆分出来L1.5如下：
具体的：
Step1. L1 ：工具输出 → 摘要 OffloadEntry + 原文存入 refs/*.md;
Step2. L1.5 ：判定长任务边界，激活 MMD 文件;
Step3. L2 ：后台生成/增量更新 Mermaid 流程图;
Step4. 注入器 ：把紧凑的 Mermaid 图注入上下文，替代冗长的工具历史;
Step5. L3 回收 ：当上下文继续膨胀时，轻度 ：用摘要替换工具结果（原文在 refs/ 中无损保留）；激进 ：删除最早的消息，但注入历史 MMD 图作为补偿（mmd-injector.ts#L294-L301）； 紧急 ：更激进截断.
这里的关键在于：每一步压缩都有回溯路径 。从 MMD 的 node_id → offload.jsonl 中的条目 → refs/*.md 中的完整原文。压缩不是失忆，而是渐进式披露。
这里要展开去讲几个点。
1、Mermaid流程图做任务图的逻辑
短期上下文卸载采用Mermaid流程图作为符号压缩层，这一步解决的是将庞大的工具调用历史转化为紧凑、语义丰富的任务图 。
关于这块，文章（mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg ）做了个较好的解读， Mermaid有几个很适合 Agent 的特点，如下：可以表达节点、边、状态、依赖关系，节点可以绑定 node_id、summary、result_ref。
此外，重点尝试了两种格式：Flowchart 和 StateDiagram ，结论就是Flowchart 相比 StateDiagram 效果约提升 15%，在长任务上下文卸载场景中，Agent 更需要一张表达复杂依赖和多路径探索且足够自由的任务地图，StateDiagram 更适合描述有严格生命周期的对象，Flowchart 更适合描述 Agent 高度自由的探索式执行过程。
2、Mermaid流程图做任务图的具体实现
但这个仅仅是结构，还是要具体承载，在具体实现上，对应的promp t如下：
调用本地LLM，输入三样东西：现有 MMD 内容（带行号注释，方便精确引用）、新的 OffloadEntry 列表、近期对话历史，
LLM输出Mermaid图中每个节点有如下结构，如：001-N1["summary: 查询数据库状态<br/>status: done"] 001-N2["summary: 执行迁移脚本<br/>status: doing"]，node_id（如 001-N1）：唯一标识，可映射回 refs/*.md 中的原始工具输出，status：done / doing / todo，告诉 Agent 任务进展，MMD 文件头部还有  元数据 。
在形式化上，底层保留原始、厚重的工具调用结果（refs/*.md），中层抽取步骤摘要（jsonl），高层则浓缩为一张极度轻量的Mermaid任务画布。每个Mermaid图表均以.mmd文件的形式持久化存储在Agent的mmds/目录下，文件遵循严格的结构：
由分隔符包围的JSON元数据头部，其后是flowchartTD主体，主体中的节点均带有结构化标签。
其中，JSON元数据头部 编码了任务的身份标识与进度，对应【严格时间戳与元数据规则】：
1)顶部元数据（必填） ：（updatedTime为node中的最新时间）。
2)节点内时间 ：如果合并了多个新条目，节点内的 Timestamp 必须取其中最新的 ISO 时间。
此外也有【严格的工程底线】 ，如下：
1)节点标准格式 ：NodeID["阶段名: 宏观动作简述<br/>status: done|doing|paused|blocked <br/>summary: 核心结论摘要<br/>Timestamp: ISO8601"]
对应4种状态：
其中，blocked 状态是系统的防重复机制：通过将死胡同显式记录为墓碑节点，LLM 避免了浪费 token 去重新尝试已经失败的方法，L2 提示词将其称为”认知墓碑” (cognitive tombstones)。
2)全员归宿映射 ：输入的每一个新 tool_call_id，都必须在 node_mapping 中被分配到一个 Node ID；MMD里的每一个node都应该有源头的tool_call消息来源，不能乱编，绝对不允许遗漏 （Node_id和tool_call_id是一对多的关系）
3、mermaid的注入形式
当然，生成Mermaid图表还不够，图表必须被注入到 LLM 的消息数组中的正确位置，才能能充当压缩上下文，具体插入到上下文的原则是：
找到最新的user消息位置，如果它在对话后半段→插入到它之后，如果在前半段→找到尾部工具循环块的起始位置（距尾部≤30条），关键保护：不会拆散assistant(tool_use)→tool_result的配对，这样MMD恰好坐在”用户问题”和”进行中的工具循环”之间，Agent先看到任务概览图，再看具体工具操作 。
最终注入到上下文中的是一条 user 消息，结构如下：
4、mermaid画布的执行意义
因此，在这个mermaid画布搭建完之后，可以看具体用法，这里援引mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg 的论述：

第一层是鸟瞰/Overview 。Agent先看到的是任务级别的概览：之前做过哪些任务，哪些已经完成，哪些还在进行，当前用户的问题可能和哪一个历史任务有关。这一层不追求细节，而是帮助Agent快速判断方向；
第二层是聚焦 。当Agent判断某个任务相关时，它再打开对应的任务画布，查看这个任务内部的结构。此时，Agent看到的不是一长串对话历史，而是一张经过组织的任务地图：哪些步骤已经完成，哪些节点还在进行，哪些信息汇聚成了当前结论；
第三层是下钻/细节补充 。当画布上的节点信息仍然不够时，Agent才进一步追溯到更细的摘要或原始材料。也就是说，原文不是一直常驻上下文，而是在真正需要确认细节时才被重新展开。

三、最后的总结-优劣势
总的来说，TencentDBAgentMemory是一个面向AIAgent的分层记忆系统，核心解决两个问题：
长期记忆方面 ，通过跨会话记住用户偏好、项目上下文、SOP，通过L0→L1→L2→L3的渐进式蒸馏，每层由LLM驱动提取；
短期记忆方面 ，单次长任务中上下文窗口不够用，通过Mermaid符号压缩+外部文件卸载+三级回收机制，每一层压缩都保留确定性回溯路径（node_id→refs/*.md），区别于粗暴摘要，这种设计让Agent在上下文中只保留高层结构（人设、场景导航、Mermaid画布），需要细节时下钻。比扁平向量库更合适些。
其中有两个有趣的设计。一个是回溯性 ，这是核心保障，这意味着压缩不是有损摘要，是可逆的。此外，用流程图替代冗长工具日志，token占用降一个数量级，同时Mermaid是LLM天然能理解的结构化格式 ，增量更新机制（指纹比对），避免了每次全量重写。
但是，也有一些不足，也看下：
其一，配置复杂度过高，光是OffloadConfig就有20+个参数，真正用好这套系统需要理解大量调优参数 （l2NullThreshold、mmdMaxTokenRatio、aggressiveCompressRatio……），对普通用户门槛很高。
其二，LLM调用成本和延迟不可忽视，每个管道阶段 （L1提取、L1.5边界判定、L2Mermaid生成、L2场景聚合、L3人设生成）都需要LLM调用。一次长任务会话可能触发数十次LLM调用。延迟和成本是真实存在的，尤其是在高并发场景下。
其三，L2生成的Mermaid图完全依赖LLM输出 。如果LLM产生格式错误、node_id冲突、或者语义遗漏，系统虽然有backfillNodeIds做回填，但没有对Mermaid严格语义正确性的校验，可能出出错，导致Agent不稳定。
所以，适合自己的，才是最好的，东西设计的越复杂，其实不可控因素越多 。
参考文献
1、mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg
2、github.com/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory

entities/agent记忆框架详解tencentdb-agent-memo.title: Agent记忆框架详解：TencentDB Agent Memory的分层式长期记忆及符号化短期记忆设计细节
entities/agent记忆框架详解tencentdb-agent-memo.content:
今天是2026年5月24日 ，星期日，北京，天气晴
来看Agent记忆方面的进展，我们之前已经讲过很多了，尤其是基于图的Agent记忆、基于树的、基于层次的记忆方案等 ，都在解决上下文超载问题、个性化问题 。
最近有个方案很火，TencentDB-Agent-Memory，基于上下文卸载与结构化图表示的短期记忆压缩方案 ，通过将完整信息卸载至外部文件系统，同时利用 Mermaid将任务执行过程转化为可导航的结构化记忆 ，这个Mermaid的方案有意思，所以展开来看看。
官方的地址在《TencentDB Agent Memory 》(github.com/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory)，对应的解读在：deepwiki.com/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory，zread.ai/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory，官方也有一些解读，在：mp.weixin.qq.com/s/mK5WoNgJSPSpdmsUSSDqPw，又如：mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg，解读基于此开展。
先说具体结论：
其一，TencentDB Agent Memory=符号化短期记忆+分层式长期记忆 ，也就是用于渐进式分层长期记忆，以及用于任务内上下文压缩的符号化短期记忆，分开处理。
其二，在实际处理时候，这些各层也是互相协同的。分成两个阶段：
提示词构建前（handleBeforeRecall）阶段 ：召回流程搜索L1记忆（通过混合向量+关键词搜索），加载L3人设，并组装场景导航。它们作为prependContext（每轮的L1记忆）和appendSystemContext（稳定的人设+场景导航+工具指南）被注入到提示词中。
轮次结束后（handleTurnCommitted）阶段 ：将新消息写入L0，当累积了足够的新消息时触发L1提取，并根据各自的触发条件调度L2场景聚合和L3人设生成，与此同时，卸载流水线将工具结果压缩为Mermaid符号。
与此对应的，就是是目前很流行的渐进式披露，Agent 在上下文中关注紧凑的高层结构（人设、场景导航、Mermaid 画布），仅在需要时向下钻取至原始证据。长期记忆上，钻取路径始终是确定性的—从一个人设特征，到促成它的场景，到这些场景内的 L1 记忆，再到产生这些记忆的 L0 消息；而对于短期任务，则是从一个 Mermaid node_id 到完整的卸载日志 。
基于这些，本文分别看分层式长期记忆和符号化符号化短期记忆具体怎么做的，里面的mermaid是怎么做的，并最后做个总结。

包含一个META分隔头部，其后是叙事内容。META部分携带索引元数据——创建时间戳、最后更新时间戳、简洁的摘要和热度分数 。

从中看，是一个结构化的分析框架 ，指导 LLM通过四个逐渐深入的层次扫描场景数据，约束LLM的分析以产生可操作的、基于证据的结果。
二、短期记忆（上下文卸载/任务）
这里的短期记忆是在漫长的任务会话中，冗长的工具输出（搜索结果、代码清单、错误追踪）消耗了上下文窗口的绝大部分，所以，解决方式，就是上下文卸载和任务分层 ，通过一套独立于长期记忆管道的三阶段压缩流水线来实现 ，分成底层markdown文件、中层json条目、顶层mermaid流程图 三个层次。
在具体执行时，Agent在上下文中仅需关注顶层结构，遇错时再沿着node_id下钻到底层查证。
这里又可以进一步拆分出来L1.5如下：
具体的：
Step1. L1 ：工具输出 → 摘要 OffloadEntry + 原文存入 refs/*.md;
Step2. L1.5 ：判定长任务边界，激活 MMD 文件;
Step3. L2 ：后台生成/增量更新 Mermaid 流程图;
Step4. 注入器 ：把紧凑的 Mermaid 图注入上下文，替代冗长的工具历史;
Step5. L3 回收 ：当上下文继续膨胀时，轻度 ：用摘要替换工具结果（原文在 refs/ 中无损保留）；激进 ：删除最早的消息，但注入历史 MMD 图作为补偿（mmd-injector.ts#L294-L301）； 紧急 ：更激进截断.
这里的关键在于：每一步压缩都有回溯路径 。从 MMD 的 node_id → offload.jsonl 中的条目 → refs/*.md 中的完整原文。压缩不是失忆，而是渐进式披露。
这里要展开去讲几个点。
1、Mermaid流程图做任务图的逻辑
短期上下文卸载采用Mermaid流程图作为符号压缩层，这一步解决的是将庞大的工具调用历史转化为紧凑、语义丰富的任务图 。
关于这块，文章（mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg ）做了个较好的解读， Mermaid有几个很适合 Agent 的特点，如下：可以表达节点、边、状态、依赖关系，节点可以绑定 node_id、summary、result_ref。
此外，重点尝试了两种格式：Flowchart 和 StateDiagram ，结论就是Flowchart 相比 StateDiagram 效果约提升 15%，在长任务上下文卸载场景中，Agent 更需要一张表达复杂依赖和多路径探索且足够自由的任务地图，StateDiagram 更适合描述有严格生命周期的对象，Flowchart 更适合描述 Agent 高度自由的探索式执行过程。
2、Mermaid流程图做任务图的具体实现
但这个仅仅是结构，还是要具体承载，在具体实现上，对应的promp t如下：
调用本地LLM，输入三样东西：现有 MMD 内容（带行号注释，方便精确引用）、新的 OffloadEntry 列表、近期对话历史，
LLM输出Mermaid图中每个节点有如下结构，如：001-N1["summary: 查询数据库状态<br/>status: done"] 001-N2["summary: 执行迁移脚本<br/>status: doing"]，node_id（如 001-N1）：唯一标识，可映射回 refs/*.md 中的原始工具输出，status：done / doing / todo，告诉 Agent 任务进展，MMD 文件头部还有  元数据 。
在形式化上，底层保留原始、厚重的工具调用结果（refs/*.md），中层抽取步骤摘要（jsonl），高层则浓缩为一张极度轻量的Mermaid任务画布。每个Mermaid图表均以.mmd文件的形式持久化存储在Agent的mmds/目录下，文件遵循严格的结构：
由分隔符包围的JSON元数据头部，其后是flowchartTD主体，主体中的节点均带有结构化标签。
其中，JSON元数据头部 编码了任务的身份标识与进度，对应【严格时间戳与元数据规则】：
1)顶部元数据（必填） ：（updatedTime为node中的最新时间）。
2)节点内时间 ：如果合并了多个新条目，节点内的 Timestamp 必须取其中最新的 ISO 时间。
此外也有【严格的工程底线】 ，如下：
1)节点标准格式 ：NodeID["阶段名: 宏观动作简述<br/>status: done|doing|paused|blocked <br/>summary: 核心结论摘要<br/>Timestamp: ISO8601"]
对应4种状态：
其中，blocked 状态是系统的防重复机制：通过将死胡同显式记录为墓碑节点，LLM 避免了浪费 token 去重新尝试已经失败的方法，L2 提示词将其称为”认知墓碑” (cognitive tombstones)。
2)全员归宿映射 ：输入的每一个新 tool_call_id，都必须在 node_mapping 中被分配到一个 Node ID；MMD里的每一个node都应该有源头的tool_call消息来源，不能乱编，绝对不允许遗漏 （Node_id和tool_call_id是一对多的关系）
3、mermaid的注入形式
当然，生成Mermaid图表还不够，图表必须被注入到 LLM 的消息数组中的正确位置，才能能充当压缩上下文，具体插入到上下文的原则是：
找到最新的user消息位置，如果它在对话后半段→插入到它之后，如果在前半段→找到尾部工具循环块的起始位置（距尾部≤30条），关键保护：不会拆散assistant(tool_use)→tool_result的配对，这样MMD恰好坐在”用户问题”和”进行中的工具循环”之间，Agent先看到任务概览图，再看具体工具操作 。
最终注入到上下文中的是一条 user 消息，结构如下：
4、mermaid画布的执行意义
因此，在这个mermaid画布搭建完之后，可以看具体用法，这里援引mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg 的论述：

第一层是鸟瞰/Overview 。Agent先看到的是任务级别的概览：之前做过哪些任务，哪些已经完成，哪些还在进行，当前用户的问题可能和哪一个历史任务有关。这一层不追求细节，而是帮助Agent快速判断方向；
第二层是聚焦 。当Agent判断某个任务相关时，它再打开对应的任务画布，查看这个任务内部的结构。此时，Agent看到的不是一长串对话历史，而是一张经过组织的任务地图：哪些步骤已经完成，哪些节点还在进行，哪些信息汇聚成了当前结论；
第三层是下钻/细节补充 。当画布上的节点信息仍然不够时，Agent才进一步追溯到更细的摘要或原始材料。也就是说，原文不是一直常驻上下文，而是在真正需要确认细节时才被重新展开。

三、最后的总结-优劣势
总的来说，TencentDBAgentMemory是一个面向AIAgent的分层记忆系统，核心解决两个问题：
长期记忆方面 ，通过跨会话记住用户偏好、项目上下文、SOP，通过L0→L1→L2→L3的渐进式蒸馏，每层由LLM驱动提取；
短期记忆方面 ，单次长任务中上下文窗口不够用，通过Mermaid符号压缩+外部文件卸载+三级回收机制，每一层压缩都保留确定性回溯路径（node_id→refs/*.md），区别于粗暴摘要，这种设计让Agent在上下文中只保留高层结构（人设、场景导航、Mermaid画布），需要细节时下钻。比扁平向量库更合适些。
其中有两个有趣的设计。一个是回溯性 ，这是核心保障，这意味着压缩不是有损摘要，是可逆的。此外，用流程图替代冗长工具日志，token占用降一个数量级，同时Mermaid是LLM天然能理解的结构化格式 ，增量更新机制（指纹比对），避免了每次全量重写。
但是，也有一些不足，也看下：
其一，配置复杂度过高，光是OffloadConfig就有20+个参数，真正用好这套系统需要理解大量调优参数 （l2NullThreshold、mmdMaxTokenRatio、aggressiveCompressRatio……），对普通用户门槛很高。
其二，LLM调用成本和延迟不可忽视，每个管道阶段 （L1提取、L1.5边界判定、L2Mermaid生成、L2场景聚合、L3人设生成）都需要LLM调用。一次长任务会话可能触发数十次LLM调用。延迟和成本是真实存在的，尤其是在高并发场景下。
其三，L2生成的Mermaid图完全依赖LLM输出 。如果LLM产生格式错误、node_id冲突、或者语义遗漏，系统虽然有backfillNodeIds做回填，但没有对Mermaid严格语义正确性的校验，可能出出错，导致Agent不稳定。
所以，适合自己的，才是最好的，东西设计的越复杂，其实不可控因素越多 。
参考文献
1、mp.weixin.qq.com/s/MSXKfefrqM31q-7WXIqCEg
2、github.com/Tencent/TencentDB-Agent-Memory

entities/ragkgllm文档智能四大领域技术前沿老刘说nlp技术社区持.title: RAG＆KG＆LLM＆文档智能四大领域技术前沿：老刘说NLP技术社区持续对外纳新
entities/ragkgllm文档智能四大领域技术前沿老刘说nlp技术社区持.content:
老刘说NLP技术社区自建立以来，通过人工跟进、筛选，围绕‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍几个是偏向落地的大的主题，大模型(Agent、skill、Vibecoding等)、RAG （多模态RAG、GraphRAG、AgenticRAG）、文档智能 （多模态OCR、表格解析、文档层级结构）、知识图谱 （LLM构建、Ontology本体、知识图谱构建）四个主线。
从每日早报、技术答疑、项目案例精选、研究报告分享、代表论文解读、前沿热点跟踪 多种形式，以期平静、长期的对底层技术本质 展开一些深度思考和些许帮助，通过公众号文章+社区会员这两个平台，探索出了6个经验产物，如下：
1、每日一早报，从技术角度总结过去一日进展
对前一日社区所讨论的话题、提问 ，以及通过人工跟进、筛选 出来的较好的技术进展和分享，给出回顾、内容和索引。
目前收录已经从2024年3月开始至今，这是一个很好的历时语料库，可以做技术演变分析的。
2、社区技术答疑交流
技术答疑是社区最有趣的点 ，这也是引导深度思考和分析思维能力的一个很好的渠道。
虽有多有少，但一起讨论问题，可以看出大家都在关注什么，遇到什么问题，以及有什么观点。
3、索引化前沿进展跟踪
好的信息，都来自于对信源的控制，比如跟进论文、好的技术博客等，给出项目解读和对应的地址，解读的过程，其实也是自己加工的过程，会形成新的认识。
这些不是简单写个爬虫就能做的 。
4、可索引的历时知识库
积累的历史知识库，其实是个十分好的索引地址，从中可以查找社区内部文件、图片、视频、报告、链接 。
这也是鼓励社区成员能够发挥主观能动性、学会搜索 。
5、线上主题分享及月度总结
我们已经陆续地举行了43次线上技术社区交流活动 ，包括知识图谱的构建与情报分析应用、KBQA、行业微调大模型 等多个方面【但是当前技术发展陷入爬坡期，因此讲的较少的，但前面基础分享依旧受用】
每个月以早报为基准 做月度总结回顾：
6、专题课堂及专题索引
专题化、主题化索引，是很关键的，包括11个视频课堂 【涵盖RAG课堂、知识图谱、大模型课堂、Deepseek、Agent】以及27个文章专题 【Agent智能体、AI搜索、Embedding嵌入、KG+RAG、NLP、prompt工程、RAG、大模型+文档智能等】。
欢迎关注并加入社区
欢迎感兴趣的加入社区，一起共建、共享社区，目前采用会员制，加入社区方式如下：
step1. 扫描社区会员二维码（左边第一个码），并截图保存，社区资格一年有效，一年一续。
step2. 扫描添加微信，备注“加入会员群”，并发送截图，可进入社区。
欢迎感兴趣的加入社区，一起共建、共享社区 。
关于我们
老刘，主页：liuhuanyong.github.io。
欢迎加入老刘说NLP技术社区 ，社区持续纳新，加入会员方式：关注公众号，在后台菜单栏中点击会员社区- >会员入群加入

entities/aflow.title: AFlow
entities/aflow.content:
AFlow

自动搜索最优 Agentic Workflow (ICLR 2025)


论文: AFlow: Automating Agentic Workflow Generation
代码: GitHub
方法: MCTS 搜索

核心贡献
将 workflow optimization 建模为搜索问题，使用 MCTS 自动发现最优 agentic workflow：

Operators：Generate, Review, Revise, Ensemble, Test, Programmer 等，每个由 LLM 实现
搜索空间：operators 的组合方式形成了巨大的搜索空间
执行反馈：通过实际执行评价候选 workflow，驱动 MCTS 探索

动机
手工设计 LLM workflow 成本高，不同任务需要不同的调用流程（多 LLM 节点、反思节点、投票节点、测试节点、修正节点）。已有自动化方法仍依赖人工初始设计，搜索空间不够灵活。
与 LLMCompiler 的对比






























AFlowLLMCompiler核心机制MCTS 搜索LLM 直接生成 DAG优化方式搜索式编译式并行执行是是（核心卖点）人工输入不需要工具描述需人工提供
本地资产

entities/ai-scientist.title: AI Scientist (system)
entities/ai-scientist.content:
AI Scientist
An end-to-end automated research pipeline that autonomously navigates the full scientific lifecycle: from idea generation and code writing through experimentation, data analysis, manuscript writing, and peer review. Published in Nature (2026) by Lu et al. from Sakana AI.
Key Facts

Authors: Chris Lu, Cong Lu, Robert Tjarko Lange, Yutaro Yamada, Shengran Hu, Jakob Foerster, David Ha, Jeff Clune
Affiliation: Sakana AI (Tokyo) + University of British Columbia + University of Oxford
Venue: Nature (s41586-026-10265-5, 2026)
arXiv: 2408.06292
Blog: sakana.ai/ai-scientist/
Code: github.com/SakanaAI/AI-Scientist (5,000+ stars, Apache 2.0)
Cost per paper: ~$15 (using Claude 3.5 Sonnet / GPT-4o, 2024)

Pipeline Architecture
Three phases:

Idea Generation: LLM generates research ideas with novelty checks against existing literature via Semantic Scholar API
Experimentation: Automated code generation, execution in sandbox, result plotting and statistical analysis. Supports two modes:

Focused mode: Human-provided code templates as initial scaffolds
Open-ended mode: Agentic search for wider scientific exploration


Publication: Automated LaTeX manuscript writing with figures, followed by automated peer review via LLM judge

Milestone
The AI Scientist’s generated manuscript passed the first round of peer review at a workshop of a top-tier machine learning conference (ICML 2025 workshop, 70% acceptance rate). This was the first time a fully AI-generated paper cleared a human peer review gate.
Limitations

Domain constrained to ML research (code-template-driven experiments)
Peer review was at workshop level, not main conference
Lacks physical experimentation capability
Generated papers vary in quality and novelty

Related

scientist-digital-twin — the concept of digitally replicating a specific scientist’s cognitive assets (different goal: preservation vs. autonomous discovery)
agent-memory — memory systems for agents, relevant for how AI Scientist stores and retrieves research context
coding-agents — the class of agents that write and execute code as part of scientific workflows
heuristic-learning — alternative learning paradigm; the AI Scientist embodies a form of automated heuristic search

entities/andrej-karpathy.title: Andrej Karpathy
entities/andrej-karpathy.content:
Andrej Karpathy
AI researcher and engineer. Co-founder of OpenAI (2015-2017), Senior Director of AI at Tesla (2017-2022), returned to OpenAI (2023-2024). Known for foundational work in deep learning, computer vision, and AI education.
LLM Wiki Concept
In March 2025, Karpathy proposed the idea of an LLM-compiled personal wiki: a knowledge base where an LLM reads all your sources and compiles them into interconnected articles. This sparked the llm-wiki-compiler category of tools, including:

sage-wiki — Go implementation by xoai
llm-wiki-compiler — Node.js CLI implementation

Related Concepts

llm-wiki-compiler — the tool category his idea inspired
sage-wiki — the leading implementation
coding-agents — related to his broader work on AI-assisted development

entities/chat2workflow.title: Chat2Workflow
entities/chat2workflow.content:
Chat2Workflow

NL → 可执行可视化 Workflow 的基准测试 (arXiv 2026)


论文: Chat2Workflow: A Benchmark for Natural Language to Executable Visual Workflows
团队: 浙江大学 KG 组 zju-kg
代码: GitHub
数据: HuggingFace Collection

核心贡献

首个 NL→可执行可视化 Workflow 基准：模型根据多轮用户需求生成能导入 Dify、Coze 等平台的 workflow DSL
多层验证：格式检查 → 节点检查 → 变量检查 → 拓扑检查 → 真运行测试用例
端到端评估：不只检查生成的 DSL 是否正确，还真正运行并验证最终输出是否满足任务要求

动机
Dify、Coze 等 visual workflow 平台已经大量用于 agentic application，但 workflow 仍主要由人手工搭建。作者关注的核心问题：模型能否生成平台真正接受、运行后真正满足用户需求的 workflow？
与 WorFBench 的区别

WorFBench: 抽象的子图子序列匹配评估（更理论）
Chat2Workflow: 端到端可执行性验证（更实践）

entities/clawhub.title: ClawHub
entities/clawhub.content:
ClawHub
An open skill registry for AI agents (OpenClaw, 2026). Provides the raw skill pool used by skillsynth to construct its skill-graph.
Role in SkillSynth
Skills from ClawHub (along with public GitHub repositories) are filtered for:

Linux terminal executability
Structured workflow definitions (not prompt engineering alone)
Safety (no adversarial/jailbreak content)
Deterministic, objectively verifiable outputs

These skills form the edge set 𝒦 of the skill graph, with LLM-inferred pre/post-condition scenarios as nodes.
Ecosystem Role
As ClawHub grows, the skill graph naturally expands, enabling continual synthesis of diverse terminal tasks. This makes SkillSynth a scalable infrastructure — more community-contributed skills = more diverse training data.
Related Concepts

skillsynth — the framework that sources skills from ClawHub
skill-graph — the graph constructed from ClawHub skills
skill-representation — ClawHub is one approach to the skill representation problem

entities/coolpku.content:
COOLPKU
COOLPKU is a research group at Peking University (北京大学) working on LLM agents, skill representation, and AI systems.
Key Work

SSL (2026) — Scheduling-Structural-Logical representation for agent skills

Paper: “From Skill Text to Skill Structure” (arXiv: 2604.24026)
Code: github.com/COOLPKU/SSL



Research Focus
Based on the SSL paper, COOLPKU’s interests include:

Structured knowledge representation for LLM agents
Skill management and discovery in agent systems
Cognitive linguistics applied to AI (Schank & Abelson lineage)
Making agent artifacts more inspectable and operationally actionable

schank-abelson-cognitive-linguistics — theoretical foundation

entities/critical-investigation.title: Critical Investigation of LLM Planning
entities/critical-investigation.content:
Critical Investigation / LLM-Modulo

系统检验 LLM Planning 能力 (NeurIPS 2023)


论文: On the Planning Abilities of Large Language Models: A Critical Investigation
代码: GitHub

核心贡献

系统质疑：很多关于 LLM 推理能力的说法依赖主观例子或宽松判断，缺少严格的可执行性验证
LLM-Modulo 框架：LLM 生成候选计划 → 外部 Verifier 检查 → Planner 修复
关键发现：纯 LLM 生成的计划往往看起来合理，但常包含不可执行步骤或错误依赖

LLM-Modulo 工作流
LLM 生成候选计划
    ↓
Verifier 检查可执行性
    ↓ (如果失败)
Planner 修复计划
    ↓
最终可执行计划

与其他方法的关系

与 LLM+P 共享”LLM 不擅长可靠搜索”的判断
但 LLM-Modulo 更强调验证环节
与 AFlow 的”执行反馈”思想一致，但更系统化
